reason: The prompt cleanly constrains the mechanism using component calorimetry (anode–LiFSI exotherm at ~210 °C vs cathode stability to 290 °C) and asks for a targeted critique of a solvent-flammability argument, yielding strong boundary-aware discrimination. It coherently probes one judgment axis—separating external combustion risk from internal anode-driven initiation—while penalizing a common but overconfident safety heuristic that “non-flammable solvent fixes trigger temperature.” Minor residual ambiguity remains because it is a short-answer item without explicit option-based decoy structure, so partial-credit narratives could blur strict scoring if a solver mixes mechanisms.
